examples.title: Chandy-Lamport Snapshot - Initiator Process
examples.description: Pseudocode for the initiator process that starts the snapshot
examples.code:
PROCEDURE InitiateSnapshot()
 recorded := FALSE
 state := RecordLocalState()
 recorded := TRUE
 FOR each outgoing channel c DO
 SEND marker ON c
 END FOR
END PROCEDURE
examples.explanation: The initiator records its local state immediately and then sends markers on all outgoing channels. The markers propagate the snapshot request to all other processes in the system.

examples.title: Chandy-Lamport Snapshot - Receiving Process
examples.description: Pseudocode for a non-initiator process handling marker messages
examples.code:
ON RECEIVE marker ON channel c:
 IF NOT recorded THEN
 // First marker - record state and start recording channel
 state := RecordLocalState()
 recorded := TRUE
 channelState[c] := [] // Start empty
 receiving := TRUE
 ELSE
 // Subsequent marker - stop recording this channel
 receiving := FALSE
 END IF
 // Forward marker to all other outgoing channels
 FOR each outgoing channel c' WHERE c' ≠ c DO
 SEND marker ON c'
 END FOR
END ON

ON RECEIVE message m ON channel c:
 IF receiving THEN
 APPEND m TO channelState[c]
 END IF
 // Also deliver message for normal computation
 DELIVER m TO application
END ON
examples.explanation: When the first marker arrives, the process records its state and begins recording the channel. Messages arriving after the first marker but before the second are captured as in-transit. Subsequent markers stop recording.

examples.title: Happens-Before Relation Check
examples.description: Algorithm to check if event a happens-before event b using vector clocks
examples.code:
FUNCTION HappensBefore(event a, event b):
 // Returns TRUE if a → b using vector timestamps
 // Each process i has VC[i] counter
 // Event a has timestamp TA, event b has timestamp TB
 
 IF a.process = b.process THEN
 IF a.localTime < b.localTime THEN
 RETURN TRUE
 ELSE
 RETURN FALSE
 END IF
 END IF
 
 // Check if TA < TB in all components
 lessThan := TRUE
 greaterThan := FALSE
 FOR i := 1 TO n DO
 IF TA[i] > TB[i] THEN
 lessThan := FALSE
 END IF
 IF TA[i] < TB[i] THEN
 greaterThan := TRUE
 END IF
 END FOR
 
 RETURN lessThan AND greaterThan
END FUNCTION
examples.explanation: This function determines causal ordering using vector timestamps. Event a happens-before b if a's timestamp is component-wise less than b's and at least one component is strictly less. This is the foundation for determining consistent cuts.
